Imtiaz Elahi, Assistant Attomey General alongwith Syed Imran Ali Naqvi National Savings Officer, RDNS Lahore This constitutional petition challenges the Circular dated 27 07 2012 issued Govemment of Pakistan Central Directorate of National Saving Islamabad and Circular dated 310T 2012 issued by the Directorate of National Savings, Lahore whereby Foreign Nationals who had made irregular invesunent Bahbood Savings Centificates "BSCs") WLTU held entitled to profit admissible on regular income certificates only and the department WaS directed t٥ ICcOVEI Ihe difference of profit vis-d-vis certificates to the manter, The brief facts of the case are that Ashfaq Ahmad husband of the petitioner Was former Pakistani citizen who acquired Indonesian nationality and ceased to be Pakistani citizen During his lifetime Ashfaq Ahmad purchased BSCs worth Rs 27 OD OOO on the basis of Scanned with CamScanner 60V settle

No. 26065 0f 2012 Mst Sameena Ashfaq Syed Amin Al of Pakistan etc $.N0 Date of Order with signature of Judge and that of parties or Drderi OrUET counsel where necessary Proceeding | proceeding 08.05.2017 Mr Faisal Zafar, Advocate for petitioner Ch. Imtiaz Elahi, Assistant Attomey General alongwith Syed Imran Ali Naqvi National Savings Officer, RDNS Lahore This constitutional petition challenges the Circular dated 27 07 2012 issued Govemment of Pakistan Central Directorate of National Saving Islamabad and Circular dated 310T 2012 issued by the Directorate of National Savings, Lahore whereby Foreign Nationals who had made irregular invesunent Bahbood Savings Centificates "BSCs") WLTU held entitled to profit admissible on regular income certificates only and the department WaS directed t٥ ICcOVEI Ihe difference of profit vis-d-vis certificates to the manter, The brief facts of the case are that Ashfaq Ahmad husband of the petitioner Was former Pakistani citizen who acquired Indonesian nationality and ceased to be Pakistani citizen During his lifetime Ashfaq Ahmad purchased BSCs worth Rs 27 OD OOO on the basis of Scanned with CamScanner 60V settle سممتئدتغتپھتسا تسشهه Pakistan Origin Card (POC ) He died in Lahore on 1707 2012 and the said centificates were transfetred in the name of the petitioner on 02082OI2 as his legal nominee In September 2OI2, respondent No 4 denied payment the invesued amount petitioner inquiry, transpired that Circular No.F16S 1/Cert 2012 dated 27,07,2012 Issucd by Govemment of Pakistan, Central Directorate of National Saving Islamabad and Circular No DNSL SCH(Rules BSC) 2012-!4053-90 dated 31.07.2012 issued Dircetorate of National Savings, Lahore restricted the payment of BSCs to the foreigners The petitioner filed this constitutional petition challenging the action of the respondents in denying the payment of as per BSCs Ani٥ onily providing payment as would have been due on Regular Income Certificates (RIC ) providing for lesser rate of profit The respondents No2 to have filed comments wherein stated that BSCs could be purchased by citizen of Pakistan only and it Was scheme for the benerit of the citizens of Pakistan and IOt for foreigners The respondents defended their stance ofnot allowing the petitioner claim of profit Scanned with CamScanner profit profit they سمعسامسستئن تشتهنآ The leamed counsel for the petitioner has L78l holder of POC is entitled to various benefits which includes purchasing of property in Pakistan andhe BSCs are in the nature of property, therefore the respondents cannot such relief Furthermore, the already received husband petitioner cannot recovered on the principle of locus poenatentiae The leamed counsel the respondents have sought dismissal of this petition on the ground that the petitioner is claiming relief thar is only available to the citizens of Pakistan and not allowing the said relief to foreign nationals is based on reasonable classification.

Pakistan Central Directorate of National Saving Islamabad and Circular dated 310T 2012 issued by the Directorate of National Savings, Lahore whereby Foreign Nationals who had made irregular invesunent Bahbood Savings Centificates "BSCs") WLTU held entitled to profit admissible on regular income certificates only and the department WaS directed t٥ ICcOVEI Ihe difference of profit vis-d-vis certificates to the manter, The brief facts of the case are that Ashfaq Ahmad husband of the petitioner Was former Pakistani citizen who acquired Indonesian nationality and ceased to be Pakistani citizen During his lifetime Ashfaq Ahmad purchased BSCs worth Rs 27 OD OOO on the basis of Scanned with CamScanner 60V settle سممتئدتغتپھتسا تسشهه Pakistan Origin Card (POC ) He died in Lahore on 1707 2012 and the said centificates were transfetred in the name of the petitioner on 02082OI2 as his legal nominee In September 2OI2, respondent No 4 denied payment the invesued amount petitioner inquiry, transpired that Circular No.F16S 1/Cert 2012 dated 27,07,2012 Issucd by Govemment of Pakistan, Central Directorate of National Saving Islamabad and Circular No DNSL SCH(Rules BSC) 2012-!4053-90 dated 31.07.2012 issued Dircetorate of National Savings, Lahore restricted the payment of BSCs to the foreigners The petitioner filed this constitutional petition challenging the action of the respondents in denying the payment of as per BSCs Ani٥ onily providing payment as would have been due on Regular Income Certificates (RIC ) providing for lesser rate of profit The respondents No2 to have filed comments wherein stated that BSCs could be purchased by citizen of Pakistan only and it Was scheme for the benerit of the citizens of Pakistan and IOt for foreigners The respondents defended their stance ofnot allowing the petitioner claim of profit Scanned with CamScanner profit profit they سمعسامسستئن تشتهنآ The leamed counsel for the petitioner has L78l holder of POC is entitled to various benefits which includes purchasing of property in Pakistan andhe BSCs are in the nature of property, therefore the respondents cannot such relief Furthermore, the already received husband petitioner cannot recovered on the principle of locus poenatentiae The leamed counsel the respondents have sought dismissal of this petition on the ground that the petitioner is claiming relief thar is only available to the citizens of Pakistan and not allowing the said relief to foreign nationals is based on reasonable classification. The BSCs are issued under the powers vested in Federal Govemment the Publie Debt Act, 1944 (VIII 0f 1944)("Act) As per Section 28 of the said Act, the Federal Govemment is authorized to frame rules exercise of the said ٥0w&[3, Bahbood Savings Ceruificates Rules, ZDO3( Rules' have been framed on 30.07.2003, Initially Rule of the Rules provided that centificate may be purchased by singie widow only Subsequently, by amendment in the Rules vide SRO No (I) ZOO4 dated 2OءOI 2U04 the afore refered Rule Was substituted uner Scanned with CamScanner argued Profit deny سمفائ ستعستتنا ٦ان٦ لس0ح cernficate may be purchased by any oythe Jolloving namely - fo)a cifizen cy Panstan aged stry years ٥Dove; ٥nd fb)a single widow so long ته she does HO{ F8-muFH. challeniges to recover Rs 2,TT,9T97- deducted by the respondents ٤x٥٤55 payment made to her Admittedly, the BSCs are IsSutd under the Act The Federal Govemment Was authorized provide any scheme under the Public Debt Act, 1944. For the beriefits uf citizens of Pakistan the Rules were framed to issue BSCs in 20O3 which were amended vide SRO dated 2OOI2OO4 providing only the citizens of Pakistan have right purchase the afore referred ccrtificates Ihe late husband of the petitioner provided POC to thne respondent department for purchase of the afore refened certificates for first purchase in the year 2OO8 till last purchase in the ycar 20Ii The POC as per Section 1 ofthe Ordinance cسn be issued to citizen of Pakisten holding dual nationality well as foreigners who had Pakistan 0r7gin did.0{ ٥05$&55 citizenship Under the impression thiat the husband of the petitioner Pakistan ational, respondent department had been paying profits of BSCs him. Subsequently, in the case of Syed Mehmood Arhtur Naqvi (Supra) the Hon ble Supreme Count took up for hearing case of dual Nationals in respeet of Membership of Parliament The respondents in Vew of the afore Scanned with CamScanner Profft front HLIشLاأمعنi الته referred judgment reached the conclusion that all the holders of POCs were not Pakistani citizens Therefore the Federal Government and the concerned department scrutinized the matter relating to BSCs On scrutiny, transpired that the petitioner s husband the time of purchase of BSCs was not Pakistan national thcrefore he could not have purchased the BSCs Hence, the department has acted accordingly Although the rules provided that single widow could purchase the BSCs and condition of being Pakistani citizen was not made applicable to the widows before OID8 2OI bur in the present ٥ ؟٥3 the BSCs WEI٤ purchased by the petitioner herself and hence she cannot claim benefit of the rules even sotnie cenificales Were purchased by her husband prior t0 0!.08,2011.

For the beriefits uf citizens of Pakistan the Rules were framed to issue BSCs in 20O3 which were amended vide SRO dated 2OOI2OO4 providing only the citizens of Pakistan have right purchase the afore referred ccrtificates Ihe late husband of the petitioner provided POC to thne respondent department for purchase of the afore refened certificates for first purchase in the year 2OO8 till last purchase in the ycar 20Ii The POC as per Section 1 ofthe Ordinance cسn be issued to citizen of Pakisten holding dual nationality well as foreigners who had Pakistan 0r7gin did.0{ ٥05$&55 citizenship Under the impression thiat the husband of the petitioner Pakistan ational, respondent department had been paying profits of BSCs him. Subsequently, in the case of Syed Mehmood Arhtur Naqvi (Supra) the Hon ble Supreme Count took up for hearing case of dual Nationals in respeet of Membership of Parliament The respondents in Vew of the afore Scanned with CamScanner Profft front HLIشLاأمعنi الته referred judgment reached the conclusion that all the holders of POCs were not Pakistani citizens Therefore the Federal Government and the concerned department scrutinized the matter relating to BSCs On scrutiny, transpired that the petitioner s husband the time of purchase of BSCs was not Pakistan national thcrefore he could not have purchased the BSCs Hence, the department has acted accordingly Although the rules provided that single widow could purchase the BSCs and condition of being Pakistani citizen was not made applicable to the widows before OID8 2OI bur in the present ٥ ؟٥3 the BSCs WEI٤ purchased by the petitioner herself and hence she cannot claim benefit of the rules even sotnie cenificales Were purchased by her husband prior t0 0!.08,2011. Tie leamed counsel for the petitioner has tried to argue that on the principle of locus poenientiae the respondents cannot recover the amount already paid by them petitioner. The principle 5نت0. poententice provides that party withdraw representation made by It befote it has been acted upon the other party and mnatter has not yct been finalized However, after finalization of the 5 3I. steps cannot be WILhdraWI. The principle of locus Scanned with CamScanner ILشLr icaifatlnu 1زو>1.1 5!ت0لل poenttentiae of receding back the steps is available only Where action representation has becn made erroneously. As per rules the purchase of BSCs were only limited to the citizens 0f Pakistan and pcrson not citizen of Pakistan was not entitled to purchase the same except case ofa female prior to OIOB2Oi1 ` Although profits were payable at regular intervals but had to bu deternined the time of final encashment of certificates that what amount was duc to be paid to the petitioner The IatteI had nt yet become an٥ losedب transaction that could not be re-opened Therefore, thu elaim of the petitioner that she is protested under the principle of locus poenitentine is not available to her in given circumstances of the case Rcliance in Ihis rcgard is placed on فنسف تتعطتعسحك فعسسشك Sفتفلعكس فتعلعط لعسفتش فقتف (20U7 SCMR 318) and ك تعسسع فمسلك تنععلا امن$ ستع لعسعتلا Cف Sacشفت السک مله قااا (2005 SCMR 1814) the relevant portion of the latter judgment Is re-produced below: "The concepr oy locus poenntennae the power to recede nll decIJIvg SIEP laken bur principle oy law Ihal order once passed become I٣٢٤vOc4bIe 4nd DaST avia closed transachoni wa5 also laid doww Ihar Vthe order was tllegal Scanned with CamScanner Pasi
